title: Coinkite Launches Coldcard MK5: Major UX Upgrades to Flagship Bitcoin Hardware Wallet
summary: Coinkite has released the Coldcard MK5, building on the MK4's dual secure element security with a larger Gorilla Glass screen, redesigned buttons, and improved NFC capabilities for smoother Bitcoin transactions.
body:
Coinkite, a prominent Bitcoin hardware wallet manufacturer, has released the Coldcard MK5. This new model improves on the MK4's already robust security features, which included dual secure elements, by adding a larger Gorilla Glass screen and redesigned buttons. The MK5 also features enhanced NFC capabilities, making it easier to conduct Bitcoin transactions securely.

The Coldcard MK5's larger screen and improved NFC are designed to streamline the user experience, making it more intuitive for both new and experienced Bitcoin users. The device maintains the dual secure element architecture of its predecessor, ensuring that private keys remain secure even if the device is tampered with. Coinkite has also redesigned the buttons for better ergonomics and durability.

For everyday Bitcoin users, the Coldcard MK5 offers a more user-friendly way to manage Bitcoin holdings securely. The larger screen makes it easier to read transaction details, while the improved NFC allows for quicker and more convenient transactions. This is particularly useful for those who prioritize self-custody and want to ensure their Bitcoin is protected from online threats.

The Coldcard MK5 is now available for purchase, and Bitcoin enthusiasts are encouraged to check out the new features and improvements. For those interested in upgrading their hardware wallet, the MK5's enhanced user experience and security features make it a compelling choice. Keep an eye on Coinkite's official channels for more details and updates on the MK5.
